title: SLIP transport: test_reset doesn't synchronize with RX thread
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/transport/slip_transport.c
Lines: 696-709

slip_transport_test_reset() only acquires tx_mutex but resets state accessed by the RX thread:

    void slip_transport_test_reset(void)
    {
        struct slip_transport_ctx *ctx = &s_ctx;

        k_mutex_lock(&ctx->tx_mutex, K_FOREVER);

        ctx->rx_state = SLIP_STATE_IDLE;  // RX thread reads this!
        ctx->rx_len = 0;                   // RX thread reads/writes this!
        ctx->rx_overflow = false;          // RX thread reads/writes this!
        ...
        ring_buf_reset(&ctx->rx_ring);     // RX thread reads from ring!
        memset(&ctx->stats, 0, sizeof(ctx->stats)); // RX thread writes stats!

        k_mutex_unlock(&ctx->tx_mutex);
    }

The RX thread (slip_rx_thread_fn) accesses rx_state, rx_len, rx_overflow, rx_ring, and stats without holding tx_mutex. This creates a data race during test reset.

Compare with kiss_transport_test_reset() which at least attempts to acquire multiple mutexes.

Fix: Either add synchronization for RX state (e.g., rx_mutex), or signal the RX thread to pause during reset.
